description: Lock sun-dried mud brick to Neolithic Jericho architecture and kiln-fired bricks to later Mesopotamian fired-clay building materials.

checks.reason: Sun-dried mud brick is anchored to c. 9000-5200 BCE Jericho and PPNA mud-brick house evidence.
The source-backed mud-brick examples are settlement houses.
Mud bricks are made from clay-rich sediment, water, and temper.
Kiln-fired bricks are anchored to third-millennium Mesopotamian evidence.
Controlled ceramic firing and kiln practice enable fired clay brick production.
Sun-dried clay brick is the unfired building-material predecessor/context for later kiln-fired bricks.
Neolithic bread ovens must not depend directly on later kiln-fired building bricks.
Early urbanization must not depend directly on a later, narrow fired-brick material node.
